personal_info.name: Yusuf İhsan Görgel
personal_info.title: Software Engineer
personal_info.bio: I'm a software engineer from Turkey focused on Flutter and Go. I build mobile apps, web frontends, and the backend systems that power them — from database schemas to Docker deployments. Currently building SaaS products and open-source developer tools.
personal_info.tagline: Crafting digital experiences with code & creativity

experiences.title: Flutter Developer
experiences.description:
Building FugaSoft — a cross-platform POS and sales management system
Developed complex SQL architectures with SQLite-Drift local database
Implemented reactive programming with Mediator Pattern and GetX state management
Deployed across Android/iOS tablets and desktop platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)
Integrated socket technology for real-time data sync and barcode scanning systems

experiences.title: Full Stack Developer
experiences.description:
Led mobile development team on Dorse — a logistics tracking application
Built Flutter apps deployed across Android, iOS, and web platforms
Implemented WebSocket-based real-time vehicle tracking with Google/Apple Maps
Coordinated frontend, backend, and QA workflows as Mobile Team Lead

experiences.title: Flutter Developer
experiences.description:
Developed Aydınlık E-Newspaper and Bilim ve Ütopya E-Magazine mobile applications
Implemented MVVM architecture with GetX and Dio for API integration
Published to both Google Play and App Store

projects.title: FugaSoft (Pos - Sales Management Software)
projects.description: Flutter front-end, SQLite-Drift, GetX, i18n, reactive programming; Android/iOS tablet and desktop (Linux, macOS, Windows)

case_study.problem: Businesses needed a unified POS and sales management system that works reliably across Android/iOS tablets and desktop platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows), managing inventory across 50+ retail locations with barcode scanning and offline-first data sync.
case_study.solution: Built a cross-platform Flutter application using SQLite-Drift for complex local SQL queries, GetX for reactive state management with Mediator Pattern, an offline-first sync engine with zero data loss, and full i18n support for multi-language deployment.
case_study.result: Deployed to 5 platforms, processing 100K+ daily transactions with complex SQL architectures, reactive data streams, and real-time barcode scanning — serving daily retail operations with reliable offline-first capability.

case_study.problem: A major Turkish newspaper needed a modern digital reading experience for mobile users, with smooth page rendering, subscription management, and offline access to editions.
case_study.solution: Developed a Flutter mobile app with MVVM architecture, GetX state management, and Dio for API integration. Implemented efficient page rendering and caching for a seamless reading experience.
case_study.result: 10K+ downloads with a 4.5+ star rating on both stores, providing thousands of readers with a reliable and performant digital newspaper experience on iOS and Android.

case_study.problem: A science magazine required a dedicated digital platform for readers to access monthly issues, manage subscriptions, and enjoy rich scientific content on mobile devices.
case_study.solution: Built a Flutter app sharing the proven MVVM architecture with Dio API integration, optimized for magazine-format content with high-quality image rendering and issue navigation.
case_study.result: Serving thousands of monthly readers on Google Play and App Store, giving science enthusiasts a dedicated and polished mobile reading experience for Bilim ve Utopya magazine.

projects.title: Dorse App
projects.description: Both mobile application and website; Mobile: Flutter; Web: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React; Technologies used: RESTful API, SOLID, Google and Apple Maps integration, WebSocket

case_study.problem: The logistics industry needed a real-time vehicle tracking and fleet management solution that works across mobile platforms and web, with live location updates and route optimization.
case_study.solution: Led mobile development using Flutter with WebSocket-based real-time vehicle tracking, Google and Apple Maps SDK integration, RESTful API architecture following SOLID principles, and a companion React web dashboard.
case_study.result: Tracking 5,000+ concurrent vehicles with <200ms latency across Android, iOS, and web platforms — enabling logistics companies to track vehicles in real time, optimize routes, and manage fleets with reliable WebSocket connections.

testimonials.quote: Working with Yusuf on the Dorse logistics platform was a great experience. He led the mobile team effectively, delivering real-time vehicle tracking with WebSocket integration across both iOS and Android. His technical depth in Flutter is outstanding.
testimonials.name: Emrah Turk

testimonials.quote: Yusuf has a strong grasp of both mobile and backend systems. On the Promob e-magazine and e-newspaper platform he built critical features end-to-end — from the Flutter frontend to the API layer — with clean, maintainable code. A dependable engineer who delivers on time.
testimonials.name: Ferhat Balaban
